---
output: html_document
---

# Poster Assignment

## Purpose and Goals of the Poster Assignment

This assignment encourages you to:

- Complete and showcase your analysis in a concise format.
- Gain experience in presenting research visually and verbally.
- Give and receive constructive feedback.

The purpose of this assignment is to help you complete your data analysis ahead of the final project deadline and engage in a peer review process to both give and receive feedback. This assignment mirrors a typical academic conference experience where presenters create posters and explain their work. You’ll create a digital poster of your analysis and record a short video explaining it.

### Digital Poster Creation
   - Create a digital poster that presents the key components of your analysis, including the research question, methodology, main findings, and conclusion.
   - You may use any software to create your poster (e.g., Canva, RStudio, PowerPoint).
   - Ensure that your poster is clear, visually engaging, and concise.
   - Most conferences will specify the dimensions that your poster should be (e.g., 36 inches * 48 inches). I am leaving the size of the canvas up to you.
   - Minimize text on the poster as much as possible. 
   - Bullet points are welcome rather than paragraphs of writing. 

### Video Presentation
   - Record a **5-minute video (no longer)** of yourself explaining your poster.
   - You can use any software tool that you find suitable to record this video. I use Zoom to make videos and I find that works well, but if you prefer a different software that is fine! 
   - Your explanation should be similar to a poster presentation at an academic conference. Aim to summarize the main points of your analysis clearly and concisely.
   - In your video, you should explain the **rationale** for the current fictitious project, justify the **chosen methodological approach**, **explain the results** from the analyses and **interpret the findings** in the context of relevant literature. 
   - Make sure your video demonstrates an understanding of the purpose, method, and findings of your analysis.

### Evaluation Criteria
   - Your poster is worth **5 marks** and will be graded based on clarity, organization, analysis, visual appeal, and adherence to the 5-minute time limit.
   - Grades will focus on the clarity of your analysis and how effectively your poster communicates key findings.

### Peer Review (Mandatory for Participation Marks)
   - To receive full participation marks for the November 15th class, you must comment on at least **5 other students' video presentations**.
   - Use the "complement sandwich" approach: Start with a positive comment, provide constructive feedback, and end with another positive comment.

## Submission Details
- Submit your poster as a digital file along with the video link by the due date.
- Your peer comments are due one week after the poster submission and participation marks will be assigned based on completion.

Good luck, and enjoy the process of summarizing and presenting your work!
